key_insight: TypiCore improves class-incremental learning on time series by alternately selecting typical and diverse samples to build a more representative memory buffer under limited label budgets.
engineering_relevance: For industrial and healthcare monitoring systems, this reduces annotation cost while helping models adapt to new operating conditions without losing performance on earlier classes.
method: Active class-incremental learning for multivariate time series
Hybrid query strategy: typicality-based selection + diversity-based selection across cycles
Rehearsal-based continual learning with a fixed annotation budget
evidence_signal: The method was evaluated on the TSCIL benchmark and four benchmark datasets, where it showed statistically significant gains over baselines and sometimes matched or exceeded fully supervised continual learning with far fewer labels.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of the selected memory buffer, so results may be sensitive to dataset shift, budget size, and the choice of rehearsal setup.
paper_title: TypiCore: A Hybrid Active Query Strategy for Class-Incremental Learning on Time Series
